title: Make a Bed
summary: Straighten sheets, arrange pillows, and make a bed look tidy.
learnerPromise: You will be able to make a bed look neat and tidy in under three minutes.
whyItMatters: A made bed makes a room feel calmer and more organized. It is a small habit that can set a positive tone for the rest of the day.
realLifeUses: Starting the day with a quick win before leaving for school or work
Preparing a guest room before someone comes to visit
Tidying up before a video call so the background looks neat
Helping out with chores in a shared living space
result: A neatly made bed with smooth sheets and arranged pillows.
youWillLearn: How to tuck in sheets smoothly without wrinkles
How to arrange pillows neatly against the headboard
How to straighten a duvet or comforter evenly
How to spot and fix lumpy or twisted bedding
miniChallenge: Make your own bed or a bed in your home today. Take a photo of the finished result.
steps: Pull the fitted sheet tight and tuck any loose corners under the mattress.
Straighten the flat sheet or duvet so it hangs evenly on both sides.
Smooth out wrinkles with your hands, working from the head of the bed to the foot.
Arrange pillows flat against the headboard or propped neatly in a row.
Fold or lay any throw blanket evenly across the foot of the bed.
completionCriteria: Sheets are tucked and the bed surface looks smooth
Pillows are arranged and the bed appears tidy from the doorway
commonProblems: Fitted sheet pops off the corner — try tucking it deeper under the mattress corner
Duvet looks lopsided — hold both top corners and give it a shake before laying it down
Lots of wrinkles — smooth with open palms, not fingers, for a flatter look
tips: Do it first thing in the morning before you leave the room
If making a bunk bed, work from the side you can reach most easily
For a faster job, focus on smoothness rather than military corners
